Abstract The landscape of financial transactions has grown
increasingly complex due to the expansion of global eco-
nomic integration and advancements in information technol-
ogy. This complexity poses greater challenges in detecting
and managing financial fraud. This review explores the role
of Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) in addressing these chal-
lenges by proposing a unified framework that categorizes ex-
